item.title: Attempts made in past to weaken army: PM
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Sunday said that in the past there were attempts to weaken the Bangladesh Army and make it controversial through various incidents and activities....

item.title: Consumers keep struggling to collect fuel oil
item.description: The government on Monday hiked the price of furnace oil by Tk 24.59 a litre, which would put more pressure on consumers already in a plight due to persistent high inflation and long queues at filling stations amid a fuel oil supply shortage in the country for more than a month....

item.title: BANK RESOLUTION ACT: Ruling party under fire
item.description: Economists and politicians censured the ruling party, led by the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, for passing the Bank Resolution Act with scopes for previous owners of five crisis-hit banks, including the controversial S Alam Group, to get back their ownership....

item.title: War shock may drain $6b from reserves: BB
item.description: Bangladesh faces a sharp escalation in inflation and foreign exchange pressure due to the Middle East war, with risks building across dollar market, reserves and external balance, according to the latest Bangladesh Bank assessment......Politics analysis

item.title: French ambassador visits AIUB
item.description: The American International University-Bangladesh has hosted a delegation from the embassy of France in Bangladesh to promote higher education opportunities in France....

item.title: Persistent short supply strains soya bean oil market
item.description: A persistent shortage of soya bean oil has gripped the domestic market for over a month, putting consumers under mounting pressure......

item.title: Add’l incentives urged to boost remittance thru legal channels
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon has emphasised the need to encourage expatriates to send remittances through formal banking channels, describing remittance inflow as a key driving force of the national economy......

item.title: Islami Bank MD Omar Faruk sent on leave
item.description: The board of directors of Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC has placed its managing director, Md Omar Faruk Khan, on mandatory leave by extending his originally requested leave period......

item.title: Cultural practices without hindrances urged
item.description: The leading cultural institution, Chhayanaut, will welcome the Bengali New Year, Pahela Baishakh, with a cultural event on the morning of April 14 at Ramna Park....

item.title: Global uncertainties increase risks in int’l trade: ICCB
item.description: International Chamber of Commerce, Bangladesh organised a workshop on ‘International Financial Crime: Identifying Risk & Preventing Fraud in International Trade’ on Saturday in the capital, said a press release on Sunday......

item.title: Suporna’s first solo exhibition under way at Safiuddin Shilpalay
item.description: Visual artist and poet Suporna Alice Gomes’s first solo painting exhibition, titled ‘Burnt Expressions—the Realms of Fantasy,’ is currently under way at Safiuddin Shilpalay in Dhanmondi, Dhaka....Geographic Reference

item.title: Malaysia to reopen jobs market for Bangladeshis soon: Mahdi
item.description: Prime Minister’s Office spokesperson and adviser on overseas employment Mahdi Amin on Sunday said that Malaysia was set to reopen its labour market for Bangladesh soon......

item.title: BUFT holds exhibition on soil, skin, memory
item.description: BGMEA University of Fashion and Technology organised an exhibition titled Soil, Skin and Memory at its Multipurpose Hall in Dhaka on Sunday....

item.title: Saudi envoy eyes Riyadh Air flights in Bangladesh
item.description: Saudi ambassador to Bangladesh Abdullah Zafer H Bin Abiyah on Sunday expressed interest in launching station operations and flight services in Bangladesh by Saudi airline Riyadh Air......

item.title: Youth Employability Summit concludes at DIU
item.description: The Youth Employability Summit 2026 concluded at the 71 Auditorium of Daffodil International University at Dhanmondi in Dhaka on Sunday....

item.title: German, Swedish envoys meet commerce minister
item.description: German ambassador to Bangladesh Rdiger Lotz and Swedish ambassador to Bangladesh Nicolas Weeks held separate meetings with commerce minister Khandakar Abdul Muktadir at his office at Bangladesh Secretariat on Sunday......

item.title: Easier interest rate for microfinance sought
item.description: Microcredit Regulatory Authority organised a public hearing and public information meeting on Right to Information in Ramu upazila of Cox’s Bazar on Sunday....Politics analysis

item.title: Walton Hi-Tech, Digi-Tech to seek shareholders’ nod
item.description: Merger process between Walton Hi-Tech Industries PLC and Walton Digi-Tech Industries Limited has entered a new phase......

item.title: Farmers struggle as solar irrigation projects lie idle
item.description: Solar irrigation projects in three upazilas of Manikganj have failed to deliver expected benefits, with all the pumps lying idle due to poor maintenance, weak management and technical faults, forcing farmers to rely on costly diesel amid the ongoing fuel crunch......

item.title: Domestic Indian migrant workers weigh return home
item.description: Domestic migrants in India, who often travel thousands of kilometres for work are weighing up whether to leave New Delhi for good as energy resources choked by the Middle East war dwindle......

item.title: Mideast war bites Filipino street food vendors
item.description: Filipinos like their ‘pares’, a traditional beef stew, served hot — but the soaring cost of liquefied petroleum gas has made that prospect increasingly difficult since war erupted in the Middle East......

item.title: Stocks manage modest gain
item.description: Stocks on Sunday managed a modest gain in the week’s opening session as buyers turned back to take positions in the December-closing stocks amid expectations of favourable corporate earnings......

item.title: Pubali Bank wins ‘Gold Award for Branding’ by Centre for NRB
item.description: Pubali Bank PLC on Sunday was awarded ‘Gold Award for Branding’ by the Centre for Non Resident Bangladeshi for outstanding contribution to remittance collection, said a press release......

item.title: EV-maker BYD opens showroom at Madani Avenue
item.description: New energy vehicle manufacturer BYD on Sunday opened its new showroom at 100 Feet, Madani Avenue, Vatara in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: Prime Bank signs cash management deal with Samuda Group
item.description: Prime Bank PLC has recently entered into a strategic agreement with Samuda Group to provide comprehensive cash management solutions tailored to the organisation’s operational needs, said a press release......

item.title: Islami Bank holds board meeting
item.description: Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC on Sunday held a meeting of its board of directors at Islami Bank Tower, said a press release......

item.title: Bank Asia holds 13th EGM
item.description: Bank Asia PLC on Sunday convened its 13th extraordinary general meeting through a digital platform, said a press release......

item.title: Shwapno opens outlet at Maghbazar
item.description: Retail chain shop Shwapno on Sunday launched a new outlet at Maghbazar in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: StanChart, MetLife recognise top bancassurance performers
item.description: Standard Chartered Bangladesh and MetLife Bangladesh have recently recognised outstanding bancassurance performers at an event titled ‘Bancassurance Champions Night,’ said a press release......Politics analysis

item.title: Sakib elected chair of SJIB Securities
item.description: Khandaker Sakib Ahmed has been elected chairman of Shahjalal Islami Bank Securities Limited in its 92nd meeting held on April 8, said a press release......

item.title: Rethinking import duties on books
item.description: IN BANGLADESH, anyone who has ever tried to order a book from abroad knows the familiar sinking feeling. Importing a book can feel less like a routine purchase and more like navigating an unnecessarily complicated system. This burden is not incidental; it is embedded within the country’s tax structure. When a student, researcher, or general reader orders......

item.title: New windows into 1971
item.description: HISTORICAL research on the Liberation War of 1971 has long been constrained by uneven access to primary sources. Much of what transpired during those nine months remains under documented in Bangladeshi institutional archives, while critical evidence lies scattered across collections in the West, often inaccessible to local researchers. The recent digitisation of the......Politics analysis

item.title: Lessons from Sangrain mythology
item.description: SANGRAIN is a festival that typically takes place in mid-April, marking the transition from the old year to the new one. The celebration usually lasts 4 to 5 days, culminating on New Year’s Day. A key feature of Sangrain is the symbolic throwing of water, which represents the washing away of sins and bad luck from the previous year. During the first four days, people......

item.title: Land governance at critical juncture
item.description: FOR a long time, land and property management in Bangladesh has been trapped in a vicious cycle of complexity, harassment and irregularities. Citizens seeking land-related services frequently face administrative hurdles, unnecessary delays, the dominance of brokers and various forms of corruption. Deed registration — the primary legal basis for property transfer......

item.title: External shocks and internal weaknesses
item.description: BANGLADESH’S economic journey over the past decade has been illustrious, marking it as a development success story, characterised by steady growth, poverty reduction and resilience in the face of global disruptions. Yet, recent changes suggest that this chronicle is being tested more severely than before. External shocks, particularly those stemming from geopolitical......

item.title: A bad bank law with worse impact
item.description: THE Bank Resolution Act 2026, which the parliament passed on April 10, appears to be an odious piece of legislation and is, therefore, unacceptable. The law creates the scope for previous owners of five crisis-stricken banks, the S Alam Group and the Nassa Group, to reclaim bank ownership on payment of an amount far less than what they took away from the banks.......

item.title: Mob violence continues apace
item.description: UNABATED mob violence shows that the government’s commitment to restoring the rule of law remains rhetoric. Yet, in another incident of mob violence in Kushtia on April 11, a spiritual leader, referred to as a pir, was killed and at least four others were injured after unidentified people vandalised and set fire to Shamim Baba’s durbar sharif at Philipnagar in the district......

item.title: Haiti fort stampede kills 30
item.description: A stampede at an historical fortress in Haiti has killed at least 30 people, a government minister said on Sunday, with the crush reportedly involving several young visitors....

item.title: Extortion case filed against ex-Juba Dal leader
item.description: An extortion case was filed against a former leader of Jatiyatabadi Juba Dal, the youth wing of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party, along with seven or eight unidentified individuals on Saturday night for allegedly demanding Tk 5 lakh from a hospital in Dhaka....Politics analysis

item.title: Former DGFI chief Mamun remanded afresh in murder case
item.description: A Dhaka court on Sunday placed former director general of the Directorate General of Forces Intelligence, retired Lieutenant General Sheikh Mamun Khaled, on a one-day remand in a case lodged over the killing of BNP activist Makbul in 2022....

item.title: Former DGFI director Afzal remanded again in murder case
item.description: A Dhaka court on Sunday placed former DGFI director and dismissed Lieutenant Colonel Md Afzal Naser on a two-day remand in a case lodged over the killing of BNP activist Makbul in 2022....

item.title: Burnt body found on Mouchak flyover in city
item.description: The police on early Sunday recovered a burnt body from Moghbazar-Mouchak flyover in the capital’s Shahjahanpur area....

item.title: BNP leader fined for filing false case against AL activists
item.description: A metropolitan magistrate court in Rajshahi on Sunday fined a Bangladesh Nationalist Party leader Tk 1 lakh for filing a false case against the leaders and activists of Awami League, whosepoliticalactivities are now suspended....Geographic Reference

item.title: Info minister urges expatriates to send remittance thru legal channels
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Sunday urged expatriate Bangladeshis to send remittances through formal banking channels....

item.title: DU Baishakhi Shobhajatra to begin at 9:00am
item.description: Dhaka University will celebrate the Bengali New Year 1433 on Tuesday with elaborate programmes, as all preparations have already been completed amid a festive atmosphere on the campus....

item.title: Man held with gold bars in Chuadanga
item.description: The Border Guard Bangladesh arrested a suspected smuggler along with four gold bars during an operation along the Darshana border in Chuadanga district on Saturday night....

item.title: PM urges party people to help maintain law, order
item.description: Prime minister and Bangladesh Nationalist Party chairman Tarique Rahman on Sunday urged his party leaders and activists to assist the local administration and the law enforcement agencies to maintain peace and order across the country....Politics

item.title: Residential status of three students suspended at RU
item.description: The authorities of Shaheed Ziaur Rahman Hall at Rajshahi University on Sunday suspended the residential status of three students, including a hall union leader, over an unauthorised late-night room search following allegations of female presence....

item.title: Two newly elected lawmakers take oath
item.description: Two winners of the April 9 elections, Rezaul Karim Badsha for the Bogura-6 constituency and Md Mahmudul Haque Rubel for the Sherpur-3 constituency, took oath as members of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad on Sunday....

item.title: EC to publish updated voter list on May 14
item.description: The election commission will publish the updated final voter list on May 14, EC director for public relations and information officer Md Ruhul Amin Mallick said on Sunday....

item.title: Teacher dies of ‘heart attack’ while waiting for fuel
item.description: A school teacher died after suffering a heart attack reportedly while waiting in a queue to buy fuel at a filling station in Rangpur Saturday night....

item.title: Zafrullah Chy remembered
item.description: The Gono Bishwabidyalay held a discussion and prayer session, marking the third anniversary of death of freedom fighter, public health activist and visionary of the university Dr Zafrullah Chowdhury, at the university at Savar in Dhaka on Sunday....Geographic Reference

item.title: KILLING PIR IN MOB ATTACK: No case filed, none arrested
item.description: More than 24 hours after a pir was killed in a mob attack at his Durbar Sharif in Philipnagar village of Daulatpur upazila in Kushtia, no case has been filed and no arrests have been made, despite video footage of the violence circulating widely....

item.title: BMU holds roundtable on challenges of preventing measles resurgence
item.description: The Bangladesh Medical University held a CME and roundtable titled Re-emergence of Measles: Challenges in Prevention and Management at the Shaheed Dr Milton Hall of BMU in Dhaka on Sunday....

item.title: UGC chairman calls for engaging youths in creative work
item.description: Chairman of the University Grants Commission of Bangladesh Professor Mamun Ahmed has said that it is crucial to keep young people engaged in the practice of healthy competition, creative works and patriotism....Politics analysis

item.title: Artemis crew urges unity on ‘lifeboat’ Earth
item.description: Artemis II astronauts expressed awe on Saturday over their record-setting lunar flyby mission, urging unity on Earth after witnessing the planet’s isolation like a ‘lifeboat’ in space....

item.title: ICT jails Juba League leader for contempt of court
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Sunday sentenced a Dhaka-based Juba League leader, MH Patowary Babu, to two months’ imprisonment for contempt of court over posting ‘false and provocative content’ about the judges and the tribunal on Facebook....

item.title: Online cheating goes unabated
item.description: Many people are losing money and assets or being trapped in debt because of online fraudulence as they try to make money in shortcuts. ...

item.title: Ukraine, Russia accuse each other of Easter truce violations
item.description: Ukraine and Russia accused each other on Sunday of violating a truce in place for Orthodox Easter thousands of times, as the war dragged through its fifth year....

item.title: Govt urges Iran to ensure safe Hormuz passage for vessels bound for Bangladesh
item.description: Bangladesh on Sunday urged Tehran to facilitate safe passage of Bangladesh-bound vessels through the Strait of Hormuz amid violent Persian Gulf crisis as Iranian ambassador Jalil Rahimi Jahanabadi paid a courtesy call on state minister for foreign affairs Shama Obaed Islam....

item.title: Govt betrays nation, says Jamaat chief
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami amir Shafiqur Rahman, also the leader of the opposition in the Jatiya Sangsad, on Sunday said that the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government had betrayed the nation by not implementing in parliament the......

item.title: Vaccination starts in cities, 151 die with measles symptoms
item.description: Emergency measles vaccination began in four city corporations in the country on Sunday, as at least 151 children with symptoms of the viral disease have died and 15,653 others have got infected since March 15....
